table-reference.title: Illumination power density adjustment factor for a control device

row.entry: Item Notes 1 and 2
Description
illumination power density adjustment factor
Demand-operated controls
In a toilet or change room, other than a public toilet, in a Class 6 building
Demand-operated controls
Where a group of light fittings serving less than 100 m2 is controlled by one or more detectors
Demand-operated controls
Where a group of light fittings serving 100 m2 or more is controlled by one or more detectors
Programmable dimming system Note 3
Where not less than 75% of the area of a space is controlled by programmable dimmers
Fixed dimming Notes 3 and 4
All fittings with fixed dimming
Whichever is greater of (a) 0.5; or (b) 0.2+0.8L where L = the illuminance turndown for the fixed dimming.
Lumen depreciation dimming Note 3
All fittings with lumen depreciation dimming
Two stage sensor - equipped lights with minimum power of 30 % of peak power or less
Fire stairs and other spaces not used for regular transit
Two stage sensor - equipped lights with minimum power of 30% of peak power or less
Transitory spaces in regular use or in a carpark
Daylight sensor and dynamic lighting control device - dimmed or stepped switching of lights adjacent windows Notes 3 and 5

In a Class 5, 6, 7, 8 or 9b building or a Class 9a building, other than a ward area, where the lights are adjacent windows, other than roof lights, for a distance from the window equal to the depth of the floor to window head height
0.5 Note 3
Daylight sensor and dynamic lighting control device - dimmed or stepped switching of lights adjacent windows Notes 3 and 5

Serving a Class 3 or 9c building, or a Class 9a ward area, where the lights are adjacent windows, other than roof lights, for a distance from the window equal to the depth of the floor to window head height
0.75 Note 3
Daylight sensor and dynamic lighting control device - dimmed or stepped switching of lights adjacent windows Notes 3 and 5
In a Class 5, 6, 7, 8 or 9b building or a Class 9a building, other than a ward area, where the lights are adjacent roof lights
0.6 Note 3
Daylight sensor and dynamic lighting control device - dimmed or stepped switching of lights adjacent windows Notes 3 and 5
In a Class 3 or 9c building, or a Class 9a ward area, where the lights are adjacent roof lights
0.8 Note 3

ol.li: A maximum of two illumination power density adjustment factors for a control device can be applied to an area.

Where more than one illumination power density adjustment factor (other than for room aspect) applies to an area, they are to be combined using the following formula: A×(B/2+0.5) MathType@MTEF@5@5@+= feaahCart1ev3aqatCvAUfeBSjuyZL2yd9gzLbvyNv2CaerbuLwBLn hiov2DGi1BTfMBaeXatLxBI9gBaerbd9wDYLwzYbItLDharqqtubsr 4rNCHbGeaGqiVu0Je9sqqrpepC0xbbL8F4rqqrFfpeea0xe9Lq=Jc9 vqaqpepm0xbba9pwe9Q8fs0=yqaqpepae9pg0FirpepeKkFr0xfr=x fr=xb9adbaqaaeGaciGaaiaabeqaamaabaabaaGcbaGaamyqaiabgE na0kaacIcacaWGcbGaai4laiaaikdacqGHRaWkcaaIWaGaaiOlaiaa iwdacaGGPaaaaa@3F70@ R0lGODlhZgAVAPAAAP///wAAACwAAAAAZgAVAAACrISPqcvtD6OctNqLs968Bo8Eosh8yYiRzfiZ kyq5hyvPpw0CNR6+uq+gBYe8G9Gw+x0frZ4Tudwln8aqMoSirqBPWVK4XEy/Wut1e4bWvtnTlCuO F+XwEos+P0sdUta9/gbIV9ZEaFjChBeoNzgHVidolhcWiQap5VW1dhiFKLe4GZT1B0Hjh4dFKjmT idR22ZEWO9tIO2mLu8ihi6vB2wscLDxMXGx8jJysUAAAIf8LTWF0aFR5cGUwMDH9BQEABwdEU01U NwABE1dpbkFsbEJhc2ljQ29kZVBhZ2VzABEFVGltZXMgTmV3IFJvbWFuABEDU3ltYm9sABEFQ291 cmllciBOZXcAEQRNVCBFeHRyYQASAAghL0WPRC9BUPQQD0dfQVDyHx5BUPQVD0EA9EX0JfSPQl9B APQQD0NfQQD0j0X0Kl9I9I9BAPQQD0D0j0F/SPQQD0EqX0RfRfRfRfRfQQ8MAQABAAECAgICAAIA AQEBAAMAAQAEAAAKAQACAINBAAIEhtcAtAIAgigAAgCDQgACAIIvAAIAiDIAAgSGKwArAgCIMAAC AIIuAAIAiDUAAgCCKQAAAAAh/wtNYXRoVHlwZTAwM/88P3htbCB2ZXJzaW9uPSIxLjAiPz48IS0t IE1hdGhUeXBlQFRyYW5zbGF0b3JANUA1QE1hdGhNTDIgKENsaXBib2FyZCkudGRsQE1hdGhNTCAy LjAgKENsaXBib2FyZClAIC0tPjxtYXRoIHhtbG5zPSdodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8xOTk4L01h dGgvTWF0aE1MJz48bXJvdz48bWk+QTwvbWk+PG1vPiYjeDAwRDc7PC9tbz48bW8gc3RyZXRjaHk9 J2ZhbHNlJz4oPC9tbz48bWk+QjwvbWk+PG1vPi88L21vPjxtbj4yPC9tbj48bW8+KzwvbW8+PG1u PjBKLjU8L21uPjxtbyBzdHJldGNoeT0nZmFsc2UnPik8L21vPjwvbXJvdz48L21hdGg+PCEtLSBN YXRoVHlwZUBFbmRANUA1QCAtLT4AIf8LTWF0aFR5cGUwMDIGBgAAAAAAADs=, where—
A MathType@MTEF@5@5@+= feaahCart1ev3aqatCvAUfeBSjuyZL2yd9gzLbvyNv2CaerbuLwBLn hiov2DGi1BTfMBaeXatLxBI9gBaerbd9wDYLwzYbItLDharqqtubsr 4rNCHbGeaGqiVu0Je9sqqrpepC0xbbL8F4rqqrFfpeea0xe9Lq=Jc9 vqaqpepm0xbba9pwe9Q8fs0=yqaqpepae9pg0FirpepeKkFr0xfr=x fr=xb9adbaqaaeGaciGaaiaabeqaamaabaabaaGcbaGaamyqaaaa@36BD@ R0lGODlhEAARAPAAAP///wAAACwAAAAAEAARAAACH4SPqcvtGN4JUcb6rjW41b5QotOBSemd4QiI pgTHSgEAIf8LTWF0aFR5cGUwMDHJBQEABwdEU01UNwABE1dpbkFsbEJhc2ljQ29kZVBhZ2VzABEF VGltZXMgTmV3IFJvbWFuABEDU3ltYm9sABEFQ291cmllciBOZXcAEQRNVCBFeHRyYQASAAghL0WP RC9BUPQQD0dfQVDyHx5BUPQVD0EA9EX0JfSPQl9BAPQQD0NfQQD0j0X0Kl9I9I9BAPQQD0D0j0F/ SPQQD0EqX0RfRfRfRfRfQQ8MAQABAAECAgICAAIAAQEBAAMAAQAEAAAKAQACAINBAAAAACH/C01h dGhUeXBlMDAzwTw/eG1sIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMCI/PjwhLS0gTWF0aFR5cGVAVHJhbnNsYXRvckA1 QDVATWF0aE1MMiAoQ2xpcGJvYXJkKS50ZGxATWF0aE1MIDIuMCAoQ2xpcGJvYXJkKUAgLS0+PG1h dGggeG1sbnM9J2h0dHA6Ly93d3cudzMub3JnLzE5OTgvTWF0aC9NYXRoTUwnPjxtaT5BPC9taT48 L21hdGg+PCEtLSBNYXRoVHlwZUBFbmRANUA1QCAtLT4AIf8LTWF0aFR5cGUwMDIGAgAAAAAAADs= is the lowest applicable illumination power density adjustment factor; and
B MathType@MTEF@5@5@+= feaahCart1ev3aqatCvAUfeBSjuyZL2yd9gzLbvyNv2CaerbuLwBLn hiov2DGi1BTfMBaeXatLxBI9gBaerbd9wDYLwzYbItLDharqqtubsr 4rNCHbGeaGqiVu0Je9sqqrpepC0xbbL8F4rqqrFfpeea0xe9Lq=Jc9 vqaqpepm0xbba9pwe9Q8fs0=yqaqpepae9pg0FirpepeKkFr0xfr=x fr=xb9adbaqaaeGaciGaaiaabeqaamaabaabaaGcbaGaamOqaaaa@36BE@ R0lGODlhEAARAPAAAP///wAAACwAAAAAEAARAAACIISPqcvdAaEz4VR3QV57q+x90dRhSHimTLlG 0gTHMlIAACH/C01hdGhUeXBlMDAxyQUBAAcHRFNNVDcAARNXaW5BbGxCYXNpY0NvZGVQYWdlcwAR BVRpbWVzIE5ldyBSb21hbgARA1N5bWJvbAARBUNvdXJpZXIgTmV3ABEETVQgRXh0cmEAEgAIIS9F j0QvQVD0EA9HX0FQ8h8eQVD0FQ9BAPRF9CX0j0JfQQD0EA9DX0EA9I9F9CpfSPSPQQD0EA9A9I9B f0j0EA9BKl9EX0X0X0X0X0EPDAEAAQABAgICAgACAAEBAQADAAEABAAACgEAAgCDQgAAAAAh/wtN YXRoVHlwZTAwM8E8P3htbCB2ZXJzaW9uPSIxLjAiPz48IS0tIE1hdGhUeXBlQFRyYW5zbGF0b3JA NUA1QE1hdGhNTDIgKENsaXBib2FyZCkudGRsQE1hdGhNTCAyLjAgKENsaXBib2FyZClAIC0tPjxt YXRoIHhtbG5zPSdodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8xOTk4L01hdGgvTWF0aE1MJz48bWk+QjwvbWk+ PC9tYXRoPjwhLS0gTWF0aFR5cGVARW5kQDVANUAgLS0+ACH/C01hdGhUeXBlMDAyBgIAAAAAAAA7 is the second lowest applicable illumination power density adjustment factor.
The adjustment factor does not apply to tungsten, halogen or other incandescent sources.
Includes luminaires with a pre-programmed function which provides dimming from ON to OFF (one-stage dimming).
For lighting controlled by daylight sensors, the illumination power density adjustment factor is only applied between 8:00 am and 7:00 pm.
